data.title: Last Minute-Thought: Goldman Sachs Weighs In on Intel Stock Ahead of Q2 Earnings
data.description:
Investors have really bought into Intel's (NASDAQ:INTC) turnaround story, making the chip giant one of 2026's biggest success stories. Even after the recent pullback, the shares still show year-to-date gains of 181%.

Claim 55% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock powerful investing tools with TipRanks Premium to make smarter, more confident investment decisions
  • Subscribe to TipRanks Smart Investor Newsletter, and discover new investing opportunities with data-backed stock picks

Nevertheless, the market appears to have gotten nervous about high-flying chip stocks, with the selloff triggered by a mix of elevated valuations, profit-taking and questions about whether the current pace of AI infrastructure investment can be sustained over the long term.

However, heading into today's Q2 earnings release, Goldman Sachs' James Schneider, an analyst ranked among the top 4% on Wall Street, sees a "constructive setup" for the stock.

The company continues to reach important milestones in its foundry business, while customer engagements are progressing in a positive way. Schneider also sees a favorable backdrop for Intel's server CPU business, with demand benefiting from the emergence of agentic AI. "Overall," the 5-star analyst added, "while we could see some near-term transitory headwinds, we believe the stock continues to trade on a structural turnaround thesis which we expect to stay intact post the print."

As for the results, Schneider expects a solid quarter and guide. The analyst sees "modest upside" to the Q2 results, while the Q3 guide is expected to be broadly in line with consensus. The analyst thinks strength in server CPUs will be partly offset by softer PC trends.

Key areas of investor attention are likely to include Intel's progress with external foundry customers, and the potential upside from advanced packaging. For the former, Schneider expects the company to provide additional details on external customer announcements for its foundry business, which are anticipated in the second half of 2026. As for the latter, there should be an update on advanced packaging opportunities amid continued strength in AI-related spending.

Server CPU demand is another potential catalyst, with agentic AI supporting solid demand and potentially providing further near-term upside to revenue. Investors are also likely to seek greater clarity on Intel's market-share outlook and supply availability.

Lastly, gross margins will also remain an important area of focus. With Intel continuing to ramp its 18A and 14A process nodes, Schneider expects investors to pay close attention to the factors that could pressure or support gross margins during the second half of the year.

Bottom line, Schneider isn't quite ready to recommend buying INTC shares, keeping a Neutral rating. Still, his $150 price target points to 45% upside over the next 12 months. (To watch Schneider's track record, click here)

The Street's average price target is a more modest $114.93, a figure implying 12-month share gains of 11%. On the rating front, based on 22 Holds, 10 Buys and 2 Sells, the analyst consensus views INTC as a Hold. (See INTC stock forecast)
data.author: Marty Shtrubel

data.title: Elon Musk Warned SpaceX Short Sellers — But Their Gains Just Hit $15.5 Billion
data.description:
SpaceX (SPCX) stock fell over 2% on Thursday, extending a sharp drop that has pushed the shares below its $135 IPO price. The decline has handed short sellers an estimated $15.5 billion in paper gains through July 22, according to Ortex data cited by Reuters. That comes despite CEO Elon Musk's recent warning that firms with large short positions in SpaceX have a "very low" chance of surviving over time.

Claim 55% Off TipRanks

High conviction SPCX bulls now have this Tradr ETF

Despite Musk's warning, short sellers have continued betting against SpaceX as the stock has fallen.

Short Sellers Keep Betting Against SpaceX

Short selling is a bet that a stock will fall. A short seller borrows shares and sells them, hoping to buy them back later at a lower price and pocket the difference.

Ortex estimates that nearly 196 million SpaceX shares were sold short in late July, or about 31% of the shares available for trading. That is up sharply from roughly 40 million shares around the time of the IPO. Those bets have paid off so far. Short sellers' estimated paper gains stood at $8.7 billion on July 16. By July 22, that figure had climbed to $15.5 billion.

SpaceX went public at $135 per share on June 11 and surged as high as $225.64 soon after. The stock has since dropped to around $114, wiping out much of its early rally.

Why the Short Trade Could Still Be Risky

The growing short position cuts both ways. If SpaceX keeps falling, short sellers could add to their gains. But with so many shares now sold short, a sudden rally could quickly turn against them.

If SPCX rises sharply, some short sellers may rush to buy shares to close their positions and limit losses. That buying could push the stock even higher, creating what is known as a short squeeze.

This helps explain Musk's warning. He believes SpaceX's long-term growth could eventually prove short sellers wrong. For now, though, the stock's slide has worked firmly in their favor.

SpaceX Earnings and Share Unlock Come Next

SpaceX is set to report its first quarterly results as a public company on August 4. Investors will be watching for signs of growth and updates on the company's spending plans.

Then comes another major event. Starting August 6, about 911.5 million shares held by insiders and other early holders could become eligible for sale. That does not mean all those shares will be sold. However, if many holders decide to sell, the added supply could put more pressure on SPCX stock.

With earnings and the share unlock approaching, short sellers now face a big test. The next major move in SPCX could decide whether their $15.5 billion in gains grow — or start to disappear.

What Is SPCX's Stock Price Target?

SPCX stock has a Strong Buy consensus rating on TipRanks based on 23 Buys, five Holds, and one Sell assigned after its IPO. The average price target for SpaceX is $243.81, implying an upside of 115.74% from the current price.
data.author: Shalu Saraf

data.title: Top Analyst Weighs In on Alphabet Stock After Post-Earnings Slump
data.description:
Alphabet (NASDAQ:GOOGL) delivered another strong quarter, but investors still found reason for caution. The tech giant beat expectations by a comfortable margin, highlighted robust demand for its AI offerings, and showed that its cloud business continues to gain momentum. Still, shares tumbled 6% in Thursday's trading session as the market focused on management's decision to lift its capital spending outlook once again, raising concerns that the company's massive AI investments could take longer than expected to generate meaningful returns.

Claim 55% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock powerful investing tools with TipRanks Premium to make smarter, more confident investment decisions
  • Subscribe to TipRanks Smart Investor Newsletter, and discover new investing opportunities with data-backed stock picks

Drilling into the results, revenue climbed 24.2% year over year to $119.8 billion, topping consensus by ~$2.8 billion. GAAP earnings reached $9.11 per share, up from $2.31 a year earlier – but that jump was driven largely by a one-time $98 billion net gain on equity securities (including Alphabet's stakes in companies like Anthropic and SpaceX), rather than core operating performance. Meanwhile, operating income rose a still-healthy 30.4% to $40.8 billion, with operating margin expanding to 34% from 32.4% a year ago. Adjusted EPS came in at $2.85, below the $2.91 analysts had expected. Alphabet also raised its full-year capital expenditure forecast to $195 billion–$205 billion, reflecting management's plans to continue investing heavily in AI infrastructure.

Stifel analyst Erik Rasmussen, who ranks among the top 2% of Wall Street analysts, believes the earnings report offered a much more encouraging message than the market reaction suggests. While many investors focused on the higher spending plans, Rasmussen argues that the more meaningful takeaway is that customer demand remains well above Alphabet's available computing capacity.

The analyst described management's comments about turning to third-party data center providers as "a positive read-through" for the broader infrastructure sector, adding that they reinforce the thesis that "hyperscale demand is constrained by power and deployment timelines rather than customer demand." Rasmussen believes this distinction matters, as it suggests Alphabet is spending more to satisfy customers rather than trying to stimulate demand.

The analyst also views management's willingness to lease outside capacity as an encouraging development. He believes Alphabet is "willing to rent capacity to avoid lost revenue opportunities," allowing the company to support customer demand while its own data center expansion continues. Rasmussen also pointed to management's comments that cloud demand could have been even stronger with additional capacity available, reinforcing his view that infrastructure remains the limiting factor rather than customer interest.

That outlook also supports Rasmussen's positive view of the data center industry. He believes capacity constraints "will likely persist for some time," leaving Alphabet likely to "rely heavily on third-party capacity to fulfill accelerated customer demand." As a result, Rasmussen sees Alphabet's latest commentary as supportive of companies such as Digital Realty and Equinix, citing "a favorable environment of sustained leasing activity, higher utilization, and continued demand" while hyperscalers continue expanding their AI infrastructure.

Rasmussen doesn't officially cover Alphabet or assign a rating to the stock, but his comments leave little doubt about where he stands. More broadly, Wall Street remains overwhelmingly optimistic. GOOGL earns a Strong Buy consensus rating based on 29 Buys and 5 Holds, while the average price target of $437.79 implies ~36% upside over the next 12 months. (See GOOGL stock forecast)
data.author: Gabe Ross

data.title: Why Is the Stock Market Down Today, 7/23/26?
data.description:
Both the S&P 500 (SPX) and the Nasdaq 100 (NDX) are trading lower on Thursday as higher tensions in the Middle East drive both energy prices and inflation fears higher.

Claim 55% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock powerful investing tools with TipRanks Premium to make smarter, more confident investment decisions
  • Subscribe to TipRanks Smart Investor Newsletter, and discover new investing opportunities with data-backed stock picks

Brent crude (BZ), the global oil benchmark, is back above $100 per barrel for the first time since late May. That came after the Houthi military group said it struck two Saudi tankers approaching the Bab al-Mandeb Strait. Around 12% of global seaborne oil flows through the waterway. The attack creates another potential disruption point for global energy markets.

U.S.-Iran Tensions and AI Spending Concerns Grow

Meanwhile, traffic through the Strait of Hormuz remains severely restricted. Shipowners have continued to avoid the strait for fear of being targeted by Iran.

Elsewhere, the Magnificent 7 is pulling the market lower. Tesla (TSLA) is down by over 10% after its second-quarter earnings failed to resonate with investors. In addition, Alphabet (GOOGL) is facing pressure after it boosted its 2026 capex guidance to $195-$200 billion, up from $180-$190 billion. That's heightened concerns that tech giants are investing too much in the AI buildout without a clear path to strong returns.
data.author: Eddie Pan

data.title: Tesla Has a Compelling Story, but Be Patient on the Stock, Says Investor
data.description:
Tesla (NASDAQ:TSLA) shares tumbled 12% in Thursday's trading session after the electric vehicle giant reported second-quarter results that disappointed investors. Although record vehicle deliveries helped revenue edge past Wall Street expectations, profitability came in well below forecasts as lower vehicle pricing and continued spending weighed on margins.

Claim 55% Off TipRanks

High conviction TSLA bears now have this Tradr ETF

Tesla posted record revenue of $28.2 billion, up 26% year over year, but adjusted earnings came in at just $0.33 per share, well below Wall Street expectations of $0.51. Operating margin collapsed to 1.4% from 4.1% a year earlier, adjusted EBITDA margin fell to 11.6% from 15.1%, and free cash flow turned negative at -$1.1 billion as capital expenditures climbed to $5.8 billion.

The combination of rapid revenue growth and deteriorating profitability has long been at the heart of the Tesla investment case. For one investor, known by the pseudonym Geneva Investor, those trade-offs are part of the appeal rather than a reason to stay away. The investor believes Tesla remains one of the market's most compelling long-term opportunities, while acknowledging that the stock is unlikely to suit investors looking for predictable results.

"TSLA stock remains a long-term conviction holding for its unique moat in robotics and AI but is unsuitable for investors seeking traditional value or short-term certainty," states the 5-star investor.

Geneva does not dismiss Tesla's core businesses, arguing that both its electric vehicle and energy operations continue to expand. At the same time, the investor believes those businesses represent only part of the company's long-term value proposition. "It is no secret that Tesla is promising far more than simply selling cars to consumers," adds Geneva.

Instead, Geneva remains focused on Tesla's robotaxi program and the progress management is making toward broader commercialization. The investor believes regulatory approvals at both the state and federal levels will ultimately determine how fast that business can expand.

Geneva's greatest source of optimism, however, remains Optimus. The investor describes Tesla's humanoid robot program as "the real potential catalyst" and believes meaningful progress there could have a much larger impact on the stock than another quarter of strong vehicle deliveries. Unlike robotaxis, Geneva also expects the robotics business to encounter fewer regulatory hurdles as development continues.

Taken together, Geneva believes investors should avoid placing too much weight on any single quarterly report and instead focus on Tesla's longer-term opportunities.

"I keep seeing it as a company with a near-unbeatable moat of producing integrated software and hardware solutions in robotics and AI," sums up Geneva, who rates TSLA shares a Strong Buy. (To watch Geneva Investor's track record, click here)

Wall Street, for its part, prefers to wait and see. With 8 Buys, 16 Holds, and 3 Sells, TSLA carries a consensus Hold (i.e., Neutral) rating. Its 12-month average price target of $397.05 points to gains of 21% from current levels. (See TSLA stock forecast)
data.author: Gabe Ross

data.title: 'Both Sides of the Coin,' Says Top Investor About Amazon Stock
data.description:
It's been a middling period for investors in Amazon (NASDAQ:AMZN), with one-year gains in the high single digits. While that's better than being in the red, it's not all that impressive when compared to the performance of some of the other AI champs out there.

Claim 55% Off TipRanks

High conviction AMZN bears now have this Tradr ETF

The company's ambitions haven't diminished through the years, as its planned $200 billion in 2026 capex concretely reflects. It's also likely one of the reasons that its share price hasn't been shooting upward, as the market isn't fully smitten with all the large outlays.

That hasn't stopped top investor Geoffrey Seiler, who calls Amazon one of his "favorite short- and long-term buys."

"Amazon is seeing accelerating cloud computing revenue growth and strong operating leverage in its e-commerce business," shares the 5-star investor, who is among the top 3% of stock pros covered by TipRanks and a writer for The Motley Fool.

Seiler appreciates the fact that the company's large capex has been a drag on share prices this year. And yet, he thinks things will work out in the end just fine.

The investor points to the company's history of heavy investments, positing that Amazon tends to emerge "much stronger" from these cycles. He predicts that the spending on AI infrastructure will lead to "accelerating revenue growth" for AWS.

Seiler notes that Amazon basically invented the infrastructure-as-a-service cloud industry, and it remains the market leader to this day. However, in some ways, the investor points out, the company's large reach has been conspiring against it.

"Because of AWS' sheer size, though, its growth rate has lagged behind its peers," he adds. He's also expecting the Q2 earnings to show that Amazon's growth is both "robust" and "sustainable," namechecking the company's partnership with Anthropic.

The flip side of a lukewarm share price is the "historically cheap" valuation at which AMZN is trading. Seiler notes that the company's forward price-to-earnings ratio is less than both Costco and Walmart.

"That makes Amazon a bargain stock to buy ahead of earnings and to hold for the long term," concludes Seiler. (To watch Seiler's track record, click here) 

Wall Street clearly has no objection. With 45 Buys and just a single Hold, AMZN storms out to a Strong Buy consensus rating. Its 12-month average price target of $318.98 points to gains of 30%. (See AMZN stock forecast)
data.author: Gabe Ross

data.title: Dow Jones Retreats as Oil Rallies Above $100 Per Barrel
data.description:
The Dow Jones (DJIA) opened Thursday in negative territory as rising tensions in the Middle East continue to drive oil prices higher. Brent crude (BZ) traded above $100 per barrel, marking the first time since late May.

Claim 55% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock powerful investing tools with TipRanks Premium to make smarter, more confident investment decisions
  • Subscribe to TipRanks Smart Investor Newsletter, and discover new investing opportunities with data-backed stock picks

The U.S.-Iran war continues to rage on and has now expanded to new fronts. In the early morning, Houthi forces, allies of Iran, said they had struck two Saudi tankers attempting to cross the Bab al-Mandeb Strait. The Houthis previously enforced a maritime blockade on Saudi ports, warning of violence if it was violated.

Oil Risk Grows as Trump Holds Iran Responsible

The Bab al-Mandeb Strait handles roughly 12% of global seaborne oil flows. That puts another energy chokepoint, in addition to the Strait of Hormuz, at risk.

In a Truth Social post, President Trump labeled the Houthis as a "Surrogate and/or Proxy of Iran." He also said the U.S. would hold Iran responsible if the Houthis choose to attack again. That comes as the conflict shows no signs of easing, with strikes against Iran reaching their 12th consecutive day.
data.author: Eddie Pan

data.title: Stock Futures Fall as Alphabet and Tesla's Earnings Spark AI Capex Concerns; Oil Prices Surge
data.description:
U.S. stock futures were down on Thursday morning as the second-quarter results of tech giant Alphabet (GOOGL) and electric vehicle (EV) maker Tesla (TSLA) weighed on investor sentiment amid higher AI spending plans. Furthermore, a spike in oil prices added to investors' woes. Futures on the Nasdaq 100 (NDX), the S&P 500 Index (SPX), and the Dow Jones Industrial Average (DJIA) were down 0.93%, 0.66%, and 0.75%, respectively, at 8:25 a.m. EDT on July 23.

Claim 55% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock powerful investing tools with TipRanks Premium to make smarter, more confident investment decisions
  • Subscribe to TipRanks Smart Investor Newsletter, and discover new investing opportunities with data-backed stock picks

Oil prices surged after Tehran-backed Houthi militant group claimed attacks on two Saudi Arabian tankers in the Red Sea and President Donald Trump threatened to strike Iranian infrastructure. At the time of writing, Brent crude (CM:BZ) was up 5.20% at $98.96 per barrel, while WTI crude (CM:CL) rose 4.91% to $90.92 per barrel.

During Wednesday's regular trading session, major U.S. stock indices closed lower ahead of Big Tech earnings and escalating geopolitical risks. The Nasdaq Composite, S&P 500, and Dow Jones fell 0.57%, 0.14%, and 0.01%, respectively, after all three had rallied in the previous session on a rebound in chip stocks.

In Thursday's pre-market trading, Alphabet and Tesla shares were down 5.1% and 7.3%, respectively. Alphabet raised its 2026 capital expenditure outlook to as much as $205 billion to expand its AI infrastructure. Meanwhile, Tesla outlined plans for higher capital spending as it continues investing in AI, Optimus robots, and Robotaxis.

In contrast, ServiceNow (NOW) stock rose more than 6% as the enterprise software company delivered market-beating Q2 earnings, easing AI disruption concerns. Also, Lockheed Martin (LMT) was up more than 5% after the defense company reported upbeat Q2 results.
data.author: Sirisha Bhogaraju

data.title: SpaceX Stock Price Forecast — Can Starship's Next Launch and Q2 Earnings Lift SPCX?
data.description:
SpaceX (SPCX) stock has two big catalysts ahead: today's Starship launch attempt and its second-quarter earnings report on Aug. 4. A strong earnings report could show the business is back on track. Meanwhile, a successful Starship launch could raise SpaceX's launch capacity and help win more business. With SPCX still down 28% from its debut, some investors may see the recent pullback as a buying opportunity.

Claim 55% Off TipRanks

High conviction SPCX bulls now have this Tradr ETF

Why Starship Flight Matters to Investors

Starship Flight 13 is set to launch tonight, July 23, from SpaceX's Starbase in South Texas. This is the second try after the July 16 attempt was called off.

A successful launch would deploy 20 next-gen Starlink V3 satellites for the first time. The mission is important because Starship is key to SpaceX's future growth. In addition, it will support NASA's Artemis missions and future space-based data centers. A successful flight would also move SpaceX closer to the fast, low-cost launch system that many investors believe is critical to its long-term growth.

SPCX Stock Faces Its First Big Earnings Test

SpaceX will report its first quarterly earnings on Aug. 4, giving investors their first detailed look at the company's financial results since going public. The report will also trigger SpaceX's unusual lock-up period, allowing insiders and employees to sell shares earlier than the standard 180-day lock-up. Under the agreement, up to 911.5 million insider-held shares could become eligible for sale two trading days after the earnings release.

Is SPCX a Buy?

The bull case is that many insiders may choose not to sell. Long-term holders could keep their shares. Moreover, the lock-up expiration has been well known for months, and some investors believe the risk is already reflected in the stock price.

The bear case is that some insiders may take profits. Many bought their shares at much lower prices before the IPO, giving them a strong reason to sell once the lock-up ends. Heavy insider selling could weigh on SPCX in the short term.

What Is SPCX's Stock Price Target?

SPCX stock has a Strong Buy consensus rating on TipRanks based on 23 Buys, five Holds, and one Sell assigned after its IPO. The average price target for SpaceX is $243.81, implying an upside of over 100% from the current price.
data.author: Kirti Tak
